data.content:
Plan for knots-ee7d: scope data is already projected into knot_hot (col scope_data_json, mig 18)
and KnotView (Option<ScopeData>, types.rs:41). The remaining gap is idx.knot_head: it currently
omits scope, so Foolery's fast list/index path can't see scope without a full rehydrate.

Implementation summary for the next actor:
1. Add scope_data: Option<&ScopeData> field to KnotHeadData in src/app/helpers.rs and emit a
   "scope" key in build_knot_head_data when non-empty (mirror the existing execution_plan treatment).
2. Thread Some(&current.scope_data) through every call site of build_knot_head_data (knot_create,
   knot_scope, knot_update, knot_profile, state_ops, edges, execution_plan_edit, gate_metadata,
   query::step_annotate).
3. Teach src/sync/apply_helpers.rs::build_index_upsert to prefer data["scope"] from idx heads over
   the cached scope_data (add a tiny parse helper; keep parse_scope_data flat-payload for full
   events).
4. Tests: assert idx head JSON carries scope after update_knot_scope; assert a synthetic idx head
   with embedded scope upserts knot_hot.scope_data correctly without a paired full event;
   regression: idx heads lacking scope still apply.

No DB migration required. No public CLI surface changes — KnotView already exposes scope.
